content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 2, 1993

Thomas A. Meadows
Director
Truckee Tahoe Airport District
10356 Truckee Tahoe Airport Road
Truckee, CA 96161

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-664

Dear Mr. Meadows:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provi-
sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Do you have an economic interest in Sierra Warbirds, Pilot
Services Corporation, or Todd Aero which will result in a conflict
of interest in decisions which will have a material financial
effect on these businesses?

CONCLUSION

Todd Aero is considered your economic interest. Therefore,
you may not participate in any decision that may have a
foreseeable and material financial effect on Todd Aero. You would
not have an economic interest in Sierra Warbirds or Pilot Services
Corporation. However, to the extent that a decision affecting

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-664
Page 2

either Sierra Warbirds or Pilot Services Corporation will
materially affect Todd Aero, you will have a conflict of interest
with respect to that decision.

FACTS

You are a certified flight instructor. You are also a member
of the Truckee Tahoe-Airport District Board of-Directors. In your
capacity as a flight instructor, periodically you interact with
three tenants at the Truckee Tahoe Airport.

1. Todd Aero

You currently operate your business out of the Todd Aero
offices. You are permitted to use the facilities free of charge
and periodically assist Todd Aero's clients. You stated you are
not a Todd Aero employee. You also stated that except for the
office space, you receive no free services, discounts or
gratuities from Todd Aero.

In 1990, you purchased an airplane from Todd Aero and leased
it back to Todd Aero. Pursuant to the leaseback, Todd Aero pays
for fuel, maintenance, and insurance, and charges you $5 per hour
for flights. Pursuant to the leaseback, you receive $1,000
annually from Todd Aero.

Also in 1990, you made a personal loan to Mr. Robert Todd,
sole owner of Todd Aero. You received $1,000 annually on the loan
and it will remain outstanding through 1995.

2. Pilot Services Corporation

Pilot Services Corporation has also offered you free office
space. You stated that you have not accepted this offer. In
addition, periodically you have flown scenic flights for Pilot
Service Corporation. However, you stated that the annual income
from Pilot Services Corporation is less than $100.

3. Sierra Warbirds

Sierra Warbirds is also located at the Truckee Tahoe Airport.
However, you stated that you had no business interaction with
Sierra Warbirds.

ANALYSIS

The Act was adopted by the people of the State of California
by initiative in 1974. The purpose for the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Act was to ensure that public officials, whether
elected or appointed, would perform their duties in an impartial
manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or
the financial interests of persons who have supported them.
(Section 81001(b).)

Our File No. A-92-664
Page 3

7%~

In furtherance of this goal, Section 87100 of the Act
prohibits any public official from making, participating in mak-
ing, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence
a governmental decision in which the official has a financial
interest.² A "public official" is defined in Section 82048 and
Regulation 18700 as every natural person who is a member, officer,
employee, or consultant of a state or local government agency.
This definition would include a member of the Truckee Tahoe
Airport District.

Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

* * *

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

_____________________
2   The Act does not prohibit local elected and appointed officers
from making contracts. However, as stated above, an official may
be obligated to disqualify himself or herself from specific
decisions that may materially affect the parties to the contract.
You may also wish to consult with the Attorney General's Office
concerning issues that may exist under Section 1090, which is
outside of the Commission's jurisdiction.

Our File No. A-92-664
Page 4

You stated that you currently receive $1,000 annually from
Todd Aero pursuant to the leaseback of a plane that you purchased.
This would constitute income under the Act.

Moreover, you receive $1,000 annually in payments from Todd
Aero as payments on a loan you made to Mr. Todd. The loan will
remain outstanding through 1995. A loan made by an official to a
third party is considered promised income until the loan is fully
repaid. (Gross Advice Letter, No. A-84-040.) Consequently, you
have an economic interest in Todd Aero.

Furthermore, Section 82028 defines "gift" to include a rebate
or discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate or
discount is made in the regular course of business to members of
the public without regard to official status. If you are
receiving free services or benefits not afforded to the public
from Todd Aero (or any other person or business), this would
constitute a gift.

According to your facts, you would not have an economic
interest in Pilot Services Corporation. You stated that you
receive annually from Pilot Services Corporation less than $100.
Therefore, Pilot Services Corporation would not be considered a
source of income. Similarly, you do not have an economic interest
in Sierra Warbirds.

You have not described a specific decision that might
directly or indirectly affect Todd Aero. However, we have
enclosed some general materials on conflicts of interest for your
information.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
    Counsel, Legal Division

_______________________
3 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
